    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 42
    user.username = "jane.manager"
    user.role = "manager"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_manager_user():
    """Return an authenticated user who is NOT a manager."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 99
    user.username = "john.employee"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user

def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user

"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
    An authenticated manager shall be able to create a department through the
    UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be
    written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def manager_user():
    """Return a user object with the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 42
    user.username = "jane.manager"
    user.role = "manager"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_manager_user():
    """Return an authenticated user who is NOT a manager."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 99
    user.username = "john.employee"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_department_data():
    """Return a complete, valid department form payload."""
    return {
        "name": "Engineering",
        "code": "ENG",
        "cost_center": "CC-001",
        "manager_id": 42,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def department_service(tmp_path):
    """
    Provide a lightweight fake DepartmentService backed by an in-memory store
    so that tests are hermetic and do not require a live database.
    """
    from unittest.mock import MagicMock

    service = MagicMock()
    _store = {}
    _audit_log = []

    def _create(data, created_by):
        dept = MagicMock()
        dept.id = len(_store) + 1
        dept.name = data["name"]
        dept.code = data["code"]
        dept.cost_center = data.get("cost_center")
        dept.manager_id = data.get("manager_id")
        dept.created_by = created_by
        dept.created_at = datetime.utcnow()
        _store[dept.id] = dept

        audit = MagicMock()
        audit.entity = "Department"
        audit.entity_id = dept.id
        audit.action = "CREATE"
        audit.performed_by = created_by
        audit.timestamp = dept.created_at
        _audit_log.append(audit)

        return dept

    service.create.side_effect = _create
    service._store = _store
    service._audit_log = _audit_log
    return service


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit service for verification."""
    return MagicMock()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – simulate the application layer under test
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class DepartmentFormError(Exception):
    """Raised when form validation fails."""
    def __init__(self, errors: dict):
        self.errors = errors
        super().__init__(str(errors))


class PermissionError(Exception):
    """Raised when the user lacks required permissions."""


class AuthenticationError(Exception):
    """Raised when the user is not authenticated."""


def create_department_use_case(form_data: dict, current_user, dept_service, audit_service):
    """
    Thin application-layer function that mirrors what the UI controller calls.
    Raises typed exceptions so tests can assert on failure modes.
    Returns the newly created department on success.
    """
    # --- authentication guard ---
    if not current_user.is_authenticated:
        raise AuthenticationError("User must be logged in.")

    # --- authorisation guard ---
    if current_user.role != "manager":
        raise PermissionError("Only managers may create departments.")
